formations_blocks: Germany pushing forward aggressively in the opening phase
Curacao appearing to sit deeper and look to counter quickly
key_matchups: Brown vs his marker on the left
phases.summary: Germany started on the front foot, immediately attacking down the left and generating early shots through Nmecha and Sané. Curacao looked more reactive, trying to get forward quickly when possible but spending much of the opening spell under pressure after Germany’s early dominance and opening goal.

formations_blocks: Curacao using a more cautious restart/free-kick approach rather than direct long balls
adjustments: Wirtz moves infield off the left
Brown’s overlapping/outside run creates space on the left
Curacao briefly break with attacking width down the right through Hansen
Curacao choose to go backwards from a central free-kick instead of launching it
key_matchups: Wirtz vs Curacao’s right-sided defensive space
Brown vs Curacao’s right side in overlap/support
phases.summary: Germany begin by pushing forward aggressively, with Wirtz drifting inside from the left and Brown providing the outside run to stretch Curacao. Curacao respond by surviving the pressure and then finding counter-attacking routes, particularly down their right, before Hansen and Locadia help them create the sequence that leads to the 21st-minute equaliser. The phase turns into a contest as Germany’s numbers ahead of the ball leave space for Curacao to attack, and that openness is again punished when Germany retake the lead through Schlotterbeck on 38 minutes.

adjustments: Germany’s coach Nagelsmann changed his shirt at half-time (no tactical detail provided)
phases.summary: The only concrete tactical information in this brief phase is a Curacao substitution at the restart, with Antonisse replacing Hansen. Beyond that, the commentary is mostly narrative and speculative, offering no clear shape, block, or matchup details to pin down.

formations_blocks: Curacao low block / deep defensive block
Germany sustained attacking possession against a settled defence
adjustments: Germany immediately resumed attacking pressure after halftime
Quickly taken free-kicks used to accelerate attacks
Musiala repeatedly drove at defenders, with Wirtz combining around the box
Germany shifted into a more aggressive, chance-heavy attacking phase after the 3-1 and 4-1 goals
key_matchups: Wirtz linking play around the box vs Curacao midfield/defence
phases.summary: Germany began the second half in complete control and turned the game into a barrage of attacks after Havertz’s penalty and Musiala’s 47th-minute goal. Curacao stayed in a deep block and were forced to repel wave after wave, with Musiala and Wirtz driving play between the lines and Nmecha testing Room from range and in transition. The phase is defined less by structural reshaping than by Germany increasing the tempo and attacking volume against a pinned-back opponent.

formations_blocks: Curacao sticking in it with a hard-working defensive block
Germany pushing forward with merciless late pressure and snapping into challenges
adjustments: Undav pulled left to create and slip passes in behind for Raum
Germany threw more men forward in stoppage time in pursuit of an eighth goal
key_matchups: Undav vs Raum on the left-sided through ball/crossing lane
Raum vs Room on the 86' chance and late free-kick
Margaritha vs the goal frame/finish on a curling attempt from the left of the box
phases.summary: Germany’s late control turned into outright siege football after Undav’s 78th-minute goal, with the hosts continuing to push numbers forward and attack with pace and direct combinations. Curaçao remained disciplined and workmanlike despite the scoreline, while Germany’s substitutions and late positional tweaks helped sustain pressure and add another through Havertz before full time.
